domain_experience: 6 years building internal tools for HVAC distributors
understands HVAC-adjacent operational workflows and niche language
assets: access to brother's HVAC service business as a pilot environment
ability to build automations and later productize them
distribution: warm intros through brother's regional owner network
network_access: brother runs a small HVAC service business
brother knows multiple HVAC owners in the region
constraints: should avoid using employer-confidential data or IP
micro-SaaS should follow validation rather than precede it
credibility_signals: can speak credibly to ops pain rather than generic AI hype
unfair_advantages: combination of HVAC niche context plus software execution
immediate access to a design partner and warm buyer introductions

binding_constraints: Do not reuse confidential employer IP, code, or internal distributor data.
Do not start with a pure content-first path; distribution edge is too weak.
Do not build a full micro-SaaS before validating one repeated painful workflow.

opportunities.title: HVAC AI Ops Audit + Implementation Sprint
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope sprint that identifies and fixes one broken HVAC back-office workflow with AI plus simple automation in 2-4 weeks.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: HVAC domain fluency plus warm access through the brother's network creates stronger trust and faster discovery than a generic automation freelancer has.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Without tight scope, the work stays custom and does not compound into a repeatable offer or product.
validation_plan: Write a one-page offer and ROI calculator.
Run 3-5 owner interviews through warm intros.
kill_criteria: Problem remains too custom across first 3 prospects.

opportunities.title: Missed Call and Estimate Follow-Up Service for HVAC Shops
opportunities.thesis: Recover lost bookings and stale estimates with automated callbacks, reminders, and office workflow support.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: Warm intros and niche language help get attention from owners who ignore generic AI outbound.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The space is crowded and weakly differentiated if positioned as a generic AI receptionist offer.
validation_plan: Measure callback or follow-up completion within 24 hours.
Check willingness to pay against a setup plus monthly model.
kill_criteria: Owners compare the offer to generic receptionist tools and resist premium pricing.
No measurable booking or follow-up improvement in the pilot.

opportunities.title: Job Closeout Copilot for Small HVAC Service Businesses
opportunities.thesis: Turn technician notes, photos, and outcomes into job summaries, next-step tasks, estimate drafts, and same-day closeout prompts for office staff.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The workflow sits close to the user's actual HVAC tooling background and can be shaped by real pilot data.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: Building before repeated proof of demand would create a speculative niche SaaS.
validation_plan: Document repeated fields and actions across 3 pilot accounts.
Confirm at least 2 accounts want a software-enabled monthly workflow.
Measure admin time saved and closeout lag reduction.
kill_criteria: Less than 70 percent process overlap across first 3 pilots.
No customer requests for a productized version.

opportunities.title: HVAC Service Ops Benchmark Subscription
opportunities.thesis: Aggregate anonymized workflow metrics into a benchmark product for HVAC owners once service delivery creates a defensible data asset.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: A service wedge can create the normalized niche data set needed for a stronger long-term asset.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: The data asset does not exist yet, so it is premature as a first move.
validation_plan: Collect baseline metrics from pilot accounts.
Test owner interest in comparative reporting.
kill_criteria: Owners do not value benchmark views enough to pay recurring fees.

opportunities.title: HVAC Revenue Recovery Concierge to Automation Layer to Micro-SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Run a narrow revenue-recovery workflow manually for HVAC shops, automate the repeated steps, then spin the stable workflow into software once it is proven across multiple accounts.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This path best combines the user's HVAC context, warm buyer access, ability to deliver a service immediately, and ability to convert the workflow into software later.
opportunities.why_this_could_fail: If each account requires a materially different process, the product thesis breaks and the business stays service-heavy.
validation_plan: Get 15 named prospects and send at least 8 intro requests.
Complete at least 3 owner interviews in 7 days.
Pilot the workflow in the brother's business.
Advance at least 1 serious paid-pilot conversation by day 7.
tool_specs: Human review layer for customer-facing messages
kill_criteria: Fewer than 5 owner conversations from warm intros within 14 days.
Fewer than 2 of the first 5 owners say the problem is urgent enough to pay for now.
Less than 70 percent workflow overlap across the first 3 pilots.
No savings of at least 5 admin hours per week or clear revenue recovery in early pilots.

rejected_paths.title: Generic HVAC AI chatbot or receptionist SaaS first
rejected_paths.reason: Too crowded and too easy to imitate; weak founder edge compared with the hybrid workflow path.

rejected_paths.title: Pure content or audience-led HVAC AI business
rejected_paths.reason: The user has no audience, so this ignores the stronger edge of trusted buyer access and pilot availability.

next_actions: Write the one-page offer and ROI calculator.
Get 15 named prospects from the brother's network.
Run 3-5 owner interviews focused on stale estimates and job closeout lag.
Pilot the workflow in the brother's business before building software.
Try to close 1 paid 30-day sprint within 21 days.
